items.correct: Ideological incompatibility plus mutual suspicion after WWII — disputes over Eastern Europe, the atomic bomb, and broken wartime agreements.
items.misconception: Stalin started it by invading Eastern Europe after the war.
items.random_wrong: It began when the USSR shot down a US spy plane.
items.why: Students treat one side as solely responsible. Most historians now see the Cold War as a mutual breakdown — both sides acted on rational fears that the other read as aggression.

items.q: How was the US civil rights movement achieved?
items.correct: Through decades of mass organising — NAACP legal work, grassroots campaigns, sit-ins, freedom rides — alongside leaders like King and Malcolm X.
items.misconception: Martin Luther King's 'I Have a Dream' speech ended segregation.
items.random_wrong: Lincoln ended segregation by signing the 14th Amendment.
items.why: Students reduce the movement to one leader and one speech. The 1964 Civil Rights Act resulted from sustained pressure by thousands of activists, not a single moment of inspiration.

items.q: Why did Japan attack Pearl Harbor?
items.correct: To cripple the US Pacific Fleet and buy time to secure resource-rich Southeast Asia after the US oil embargo threatened Japanese expansion.
items.misconception: Japan attacked because it wanted to conquer America.
items.random_wrong: Japan attacked to support Germany's invasion of the USSR.
items.why: Japan never planned to conquer the US — the goal was a defensive perimeter. Students assume the attack signalled invasion ambitions when it was a desperate strategic gamble.

items.q: How long did the Montgomery Bus Boycott last?
items.why: It's 381 days. “100 days” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Montgomery police. “FBI” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the Civil Rights Act passed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1964 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
